title: Display principal ID for logged-in Internet Identity user

requirements.text: Display the currently logged-in user's Internet Identity principal ID visibly on the page (e.g., in the Navbar or as a dismissible banner) whenever the user is authenticated, so the admin ('Akshit raj') can easily copy and share their principal ID.
acceptanceCriteria: When a user is logged in via Internet Identity, their principal ID string is displayed on screen in a readable, copyable format.
The principal ID display is only shown when the user is authenticated (not shown to logged-out visitors).
The displayed principal ID matches the format of a valid ICP principal (e.g., xxxxx-xxxxx-xxxxx-xxxxx-xxx).
The principal ID text can be easily copied (e.g., via a copy-to-clipboard button or selectable text).

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ (immutable paths).
Use the identity/principal already exposed by the existing useInternetIdentity hook — do not introduce a new auth mechanism.
nonGoals: Setting or hardcoding the admin principal ID — that will be done in a follow-up once the principal ID is retrieved.
Persisting or storing the principal ID on the backend.
Changing any admin access control logic in this request.
